# Figure description: Страт_колонки_скважин_черт_49_л_2.jpg

**Source**: `36572_Smolianova_1984/raw_graphics/Страт_колонки_совмещенные_по_скважинам/Страт_колонки_скважин_черт_49_л_2.jpg`  
**Generated by**: claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## Figure Type

This is a **composite stratigraphic correlation diagram** (корреляционный разрез / стратиграфический разрез) showing multiple borehole/well lithological columns correlated laterally across a survey area. It is a classic Soviet-era бурение (drilling) correlation panel, presenting individual drill-hole logs side by side with correlation lines connecting equivalent stratigraphic horizons between holes.

---

## Coverage and Subject

The figure covers multiple boreholes (скважины) drilled in the northern Kazakhstan survey area under map sheets **M-42 and N-42**. The panel appears to include at least **6–8 individual borehole columns** arranged across the width of the sheet. Based on the header text and stamp visible at bottom right, this belongs to **Report No. 36572** (Smolianova et al., 1983–1984). A library/archive stamp reads **"Кн. № 36572"** confirming this provenance. The columns span considerable depth ranges (likely tens to hundreds of meters based on the column heights and Soviet standard notation). Individual borehole numbers are annotated at the tops of each column, and formation/horizon designations appear alongside each column segment.

---

## Key Content

**Borehole columns:** Each vertical column represents a single borehole lithological log, drawn with standard Soviet lithological symbols:
- **Dotted/stippled patterns** indicating sandstone (песчаник) or sandy horizons
- **Brick/hatched patterns** indicating limestone or cemented carbonate rock
- **Horizontal line patterns** indicating clay, mudstone, or argillite (глина/аргиллит)
- **Dark/dense cross-hatched bands** indicating denser or darker lithologies (possibly coal seams, bituminous layers, or crystalline basement)
- **Blank/white intervals** indicating softer or unconsolidated material

**Correlation lines:** Diagonal and horizontal lines connect equivalent stratigraphic markers between adjacent borehole columns, defining laterally traceable horizons. These correlation lines cross the panel in multiple sets, indicating at least **4–6 distinct correlatable stratigraphic levels**.

**Stratigraphic subdivisions:** Each column is subdivided into named horizons (горизонты) or formations (свиты/пачки), with alphanumeric index designations visible alongside (e.g., formations labeled with Cyrillic letter-number codes typical of Soviet stratigraphic notation such as **К₁, К₂, J, P, N** or regional horizon indices).

**Depth scale:** Depth is shown as a vertical scale on the columns, with tick marks at regular intervals. The total depth represented appears to range from surface to several hundred meters.

**Structural geometry:** The correlation panel reveals lateral stratigraphic continuity and thickness variations — some horizons thicken or thin between boreholes, and certain dark bands (possible marker beds) appear consistently across multiple columns, suggesting regionally traceable formations.

**Two "cancelled" or "non-productive" zones** are marked with diagonal hatching symbols (ЗЗ / crossed lines) in at least two borehole columns, which in Soviet practice typically denotes intervals that were technically problematic, caved, or not sampled.

---

## Text and Labels

Visible text (transcribed as accurately as possible from a degraded scan):

**Header/title area (top of sheet):**
- Text referencing **скважин** (boreholes) and regional geological correlation
- **"Схема № 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6"** or similar sequential numbering of sections
- References to **Павлодарский край** (Pavlodar region/krai) — a geographic designation in northern Kazakhstan

**Column headers (above individual borehole logs):**
- **"Параметрический разрез по оси Павлодарской зоны"** (Parametric section along the axis of the Pavlodar zone)
- **"Опорный параметрический разрез по Южно-Кулундинской зоне"** (Reference parametric section along the South-Kulunda zone) — or similar wording
- **"Скв. №..."** followed by borehole numbers at tops of columns
- **"Образцы №..."** (Sample numbers)
- Depth values in metres alongside columns

**Horizon/formation labels (alongside columns):**
- Alphanumeric stratigraphic indices (partially legible): **К₁**, **К₂**, **J₃**, **P**, **N**, **Q** — suggesting Cretaceous, Jurassic, Paleogene, Neogene, and Quaternary units
- **"Горизонт 1 с глубины..."** (Horizon 1 from depth...) in middle section
- **"Горизонт 2 с глубины..."**, **"Горизонт 3 с глубины..."** etc. — numbered horizons in central column
- **"Пачка 1 с глубиной..."** (Bed/package 1 with depth)
- Labels such as **"Горизонт 5 с глубины"**, **"Горизонт 6"**, **"Горизонт 7"**, **"Горизонт 8"**, **"Горизонт 9"** visible in central section

**Annotations on correlation lines:**
- Depth values (e.g., **"560 м"**, **"344 м"**, **"280 м"** — approximate from scan) marking equivalent horizon depths in each borehole

**Bottom right stamp:**
- **"ГЕОЛОГИЧЕСКИЙ ФОНД"** (Geological Fund/Archive)
- **"Кн. № 36572"** (Book/Report No. 36572)

**Bottom legend box:**
- Standard Soviet lithological legend (условные обозначения) describing the symbols used for different rock types

**Central column title (middle of sheet):**
- **"Сводный параметрический разрез по районам Павлодарской группы"** (Summary parametric section for the districts of the Pavlodar group) — or similar

## Research Value

This correlation panel is the primary document for understanding **lateral stratigraphic continuity and subsurface architecture** across the Pavlodar zone and South-Kulunda zone of northern
